behaviors.description: The conversation history passed to the LLM is trimmed to the most recent 10 messages when the full history exceeds the context window.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at a module handoff (history manager -> LLM request builder), not just isolated list-slicing logic. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no real OS/network requirement, no dependence on model judgment, no multi-agentic chain. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior text is only about what is "passed to the LLM" and trimming; it does not require real HTTP/model output. MATRIX-USAGE: Unit vs Integration row settled it (handoff boundary).
behaviors.test_description: Wire conversation service + prompt builder + fake LLM client in-process; submit >10-message history; assert outbound LLM payload contains only the latest 10 messages in correct order and that downstream modules receive exactly that trimmed set.

behaviors.description: The generated response addresses the customer's question rather than producing a generic or off-topic reply.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: correctness is response relevance/quality, which depends on real model output and can vary run-to-run; stubs hide this failure. MULTI-STEP: single generation decision, so not Workflow. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly evaluates generated answer quality ("addresses the customer's question"), which deterministic fixtures cannot validate. MATRIX-USAGE: System vs Agentic row settled it (model output quality).
behaviors.test_description: Run prompts against the real LLM with representative support questions and history; score whether answers are on-topic and question-resolving (human rubric or adjudicator); fail if relevance drops below threshold.

behaviors.description: The tone re-check correctly identifies when a response is too formal for a casual-tier customer and triggers regeneration.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: identifying "too formal" is a model judgment task; mocking that decision can always pass and hide real misclassification. MULTI-STEP: regeneration trigger is required, but pass/fail is governed by one agentic decision (tone flag), so Agentic is sufficient. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Workflow because behavior does not require validating multiple model decisions jointly; it requires correct tone identification and trigger. MATRIX-USAGE: Integration vs Agentic row settled it (real model decision needed).
behaviors.test_description: Use real LLM/tone-check path with casual-tier cases containing intentionally formal drafts; assert tone check flags them and regeneration path executes; sample multiple runs to detect instability in classification.

behaviors.description: The full exchange (user message, generated response, any regenerations) is persisted to the database with correct timestamps.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is cross-module state propagation (chat orchestration -> persistence layer) and timestamp field mapping. REAL-INFRA: can be caught with local in-process DB (or test DB) without real network/OS edge behavior. LLM-DEP: correctness is persistence integrity, not model quality. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text is about DB record completeness/timestamps, which local integration wiring can validate without real external infra. MATRIX-USAGE: Unit vs Integration row settled it (module wiring + persistence contract).
behaviors.test_description: Run chat flow with local test DB, include a forced regeneration path, then assert stored rows/events include user input, first draft, regeneration(s), and monotonic/correct timestamp fields bound to each event.

behaviors.description: When the tone check triggers regeneration, the second response replaces the first — the customer never sees the rejected draft.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is orchestration/state handoff between draft buffer, tone-check outcome, and customer-facing response channel. INTEGRATION-BRAKE(d): LLM stages exist, but behavior text conditions on "When the tone check triggers"; it does not test whether model decisions are correct, only replacement semantics after trigger. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic/Workflow because disqualifying text is the preconditioned trigger; pass/fail here is visibility/state replacement logic, not model output quality. MATRIX-USAGE: Unit vs Integration row settled it (handoff/state propagation boundary).
behaviors.test_description: In-process end-to-end module test: force tone-check result = trigger, return distinct first/second drafts from stubbed generation calls, assert API/output stream exposes only second draft and first draft is marked internal/rejected.
